div.h1: Lexer State Machine: Shell Quote Handling
div.h2: Implementation Specification for Lexical Analysis Logic

p.em: State transitions, invariants, and escape sequence handling.

g.title: Standard tokenization mode. Recognizes whitespace separators and operators.
Inside single quotes. Variable expansion and backslash escapes are disabled.
Inside double quotes. Variables ($) are expanded; specific characters are escapable.
Escaped character mode. The subsequent character is treated as a literal.

ul.li: LEX_DEFAULT: Characters are classified as word-parts, operators, or whitespace. This is the starting entry point for the parser.
LEX_IN_SINGLE_QUOTE: Total literal mode. Neither backslash nor dollar sign has any special meaning. The only way to exit is the closing single quote.
LEX_IN_DOUBLE_QUOTE: Partial literal mode. Supports variable expansion via $.
LEX_IN_ESCAPE: Transient state. Forces the literal value of the next byte into the current buffer, then returns to the previous state context.

div.p: When in LEX_IN_DOUBLE_QUOTE, the backslash \ escapes only:

g.title: Standard tokenization mode. Recognizes whitespace separators and operators.
Inside single quotes. Variable expansion and backslash escapes are disabled.
Inside double quotes. Variables ($) are expanded; specific characters are escapable.
Escaped character mode. The subsequent character is treated as a literal.
